title: The Cyclic (1925,260,35) Difference-Set Case
lean_gate_reason: Publication audit clears the packet as human-ready. Lean is now a direct secondary formal-seal lane for the short orbit-contraction proof, not a prerequisite for publication worthiness.
micro_paper_assessment: Pass at publication audit. This is a genuine micro-paper: a structural nonexistence theorem for a named Baumert-Gordon Table 2 survivor, with the remaining work reduced to literature paragraph, citation cleanup, and optional Lean sealing rather than theorem expansion.
strongest_honest_claim: No cyclic (1925,260,35) difference set exists: after first-multiplier normalization to exact 3-invariance, the forced contractions modulo 25 and 7 admit no orbit-constant refinement on C_175, and the bounded 2026-04-16 publication audit did not uncover a prior exact-tuple settlement.
family_affinity: Strong multiplier-plus-contraction residual-row obstruction. The proof uses exact 3-invariance, forced quotient profiles on C_25 and C_7, and a mixed-quotient contradiction on C_175.
generalization_signal: Moderate positive. The normalization and mixed-quotient template may transfer to sibling rows where a multiplier t satisfies gcd(t-1,v)=1 and a small-fiber mixed quotient is available, but the exact arithmetic contradiction here is row-specific.
candidate_theorem_slice: The cyclic group C_1925 does not admit a (1925,260,35) difference set; equivalently, any hypothetical difference set would have forced contractions (20,5,11) modulo 25 and (50,35,35,35,35,35,35) modulo 7, but no compatible exact 3-invariant refinement exists on C_175.
paper_title_hint: Nonexistence of a Cyclic (1925,260,35) Difference Set
next_action: This packet is HUMAN_READY and has been moved to LEAN_QUEUE for formal sealing; do not block fresh curation/solve work on pending Lean.
